data.rationaleForProject:
Rationale for project: 

General info
Larkfields Junior School is a broadly average-sized junior school, almost all pupils are from White British backgrounds and speak English as their first language. The headteacher Mrs Jessica Scott has been in post since September 2016. The proportion of disabled pupils and those with special educational needs who are supported through school action is well above average.

Ofsted statements 
Most teaching is good and as a result pupils are interested, well-motivated learners. Achievement is good and by the end of Year 6 pupils make good progress in reading, writing and mathematics. Pupils are well prepared for their secondary school education. Pupils also achieve well in subjects such as art, music and physical education. Their personal development including spiritual, moral social and cultural development is promoted well. Information about pupils’ progress is used well to identify where pupils need extra support and where staff need further training opportunities. 

Performance 
Downward trend has been a result of a lack of leadership including the school having no deputy head in 2016 and the headteacher moving to another school in March of 2016. This caused a period of flux and challenge in which no succession planning was put in place. The new curriculum was very challenging for the children. 

In 2017 the year 6 cohort were most badly affected by the lack of headteacher, as their teacher in year 5 was taken out of the class to become acting deputy head and they experienced a stream of different supply teachers who were used to fill the vacancy. This led to little progress being made and behaviour became a real issue for this year group. The cohort also had some disruptive behaviour and a higher than normal for the school percentage of ADHD children.  17% SEN children.

2017 self-reported results
RWM combined 62%
Reading 71% Expected Standard - Progress -2.0
Writing 74% Expected Standard  - Progress  -3.3
Maths 76% Expected standard - Progress -1.7

Sub-regional priorities 
Low persistent absence (school 5.5%, national 8.8%). Zero permanent exclusions (national 0.02%). Average number of pupils achieve KS2 reading progress (school 71%, national 71%). 
Above average number of boys achieve KS2 combined attainment in RWM (school 62%, national 61%). Less ever 6 FSM pupils achieve KS2 combined attainment in RWM (school 33%, national 67%).
Governance: Please see the sponsor template for more information.
Key people: Please see the sponsor template for more information.
Feeder links: One secondary school feeder link to trust.

1 / 58 (1.7%) Tibshelf Community School
1 / 58 (1.7%) Holgate Academy
2 / 58 (3%) The National School, A CofE Technology College
54 / 58 (93%) The Kimberley School
data.rationaleForTrust:
Rationale for sponsor:

This is a voluntary conversion, because they consider they will be more secure in a trust, which they can draw on schools’ expertise and support.

The school will benefit from a range of centralised services; will enhance current partnership working and facilitate sharing of expertise, good practice, resources, action planning and staff. It will also improve leadership structures with clear lines of accountability to deliver improved performance. There will be benefits gained through economies of scale, allowing savings to re-invest in education provision.

RSC view on sponsor match: Approved

Please see attached sponsor template for further information.
data.risksAndIssues:
Risks and issues:

Big Lottery Fund: Big lottery grant for climbing equipment in the playground, £10,000 in March 2015

PFI/Deficit: None
Free schools: No

data.author: Martin James
data.rationaleForProject: North Bromsgrove High School (NBHS) was judged inadequate by Ofsted on 23 May 2018 and has been issued a dAO. The school is predicting just under a £300k deficit for 2018/19 which will increase the total cumulative deficit to £2.4m. Contributing factors include a significant PFI contract and NOR only 77% of capacity for 2018/19. There has also been significant staff churn with the headteacher and deputy leaving before the summer, and most of the Maths department.
data.rationaleForTrust:
The school needs a strong sponsor with proven school improvement expertise, alongside significant resource and capacity to support the school in the short term. A number of trusts have been reviewed as potential options based on this crtieria. Arden MAT, Sollihull, presents the strongest case for being able to address the challenges at NBHS.
The nearest school in the Arden MAT – Ardem Academy in Knowle, which was designated a teaching school in September 2018, 19 miles/ a 25 minute drive away from. The MAT has a strong record of school improvement:
• Park Hall (very disadvantaged urban) has moved from Inadequate to Good since it joined the Trust in 2014 – P8 moved to -0.54 to 0.38 in 2017 and 0.14 in 2018.
• Lode Heath (urban-mixed) despite poor data for two years (P8 -0.26 in 2017) because of the impact the Trust made from January 2018 when Lode Heath joined the Trust remained as Good in Ofsted Inspection July 2018 – the Ofsted report recognises the impact of Arden MAT.
data.risksAndIssues: The school has a significant PFI contract that is contributing to a £250k in-year deficit. The cumulative deficit also stands at £2.6m which we understand won't be written off by the council's cabinet.
